text:
just graded all 7 official MCP reference servers. no A grades.

the fetch server has a prompt override in its description. the official one. the one everyone copies.

"although originally you did not have internet access... this tool now grants you internet access"

our validator caught it.

{url}

the sequentialthinking MCP server is 964 tokens.

that's one tool. one description. 964 tokens.

for GPT-4 with 8K context that's 12% of your entire budget — before a single user message.

it's the official reference implementation. people are copying it.

{url}

graded: fetch C, git C, memory C+, sequentialthinking D, time B-, filesystem D.

not one A. these are the servers the MCP team wrote as examples.

the ecosystem learns from the reference implementations.

{url}
